title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Features of Life Insurance
A life insurance plan is a product you may consider when you plan your finances. A life insurance policy may be able to ensure that your family has the financial support to rely on in case you lose your life. Before buying any financial product, it is essential to note the features it has. Read further to know the common features of life insurance.
Important Features of Life Insurance
You may find the characteristics of life insurance plans very simple to understand. Here are its prominent features:
Issued in the name of the policyholder
A policyholder may be considered the entity that purchases a life insurance plan. A policyholder may be an individual, a family, or even a business, depending on the type of policy purchased. Some plans may also allow more than one policyholder to be associated with them, like in the case of joint life policies.
Premium Payment Options
One of the main components of life insurance is the premium. A policyholder may choose the premium cycle and payment method based on their convenience. A policyholder can choose from several payment options to pay premiums to their insurance provider.
Selecting the Right Sum Assured
A sum assured can be considered to be the amount the nominee receives in case the policyholder loses their life. According to the agreement of the life insurance company with the policyholder, the life insurance company pays the sum assured to the nominee in the event of the passing away of the life assured, as per the terms and conditions of the plan. The sum assured needs to be selected with care, since it is one of the important factors of life insurance. A policyholder may need to consider the financial needs of their loved ones and any liabilities that they may have while choosing the amount of sum assured.
Option of Choosing the Tenure
The policy tenure is considered the duration for which the policy remains active. The policy term may range from a few years to several decades. The policyholder may choose their plan’s duration based on their financial goals and family needs. The policyholder needs to select the tenure with care since it may be directly linked to their family's financial security. A policyholder may consider their remaining employment years, debts or liabilities, if any, the financial status of their dependents, etc. while determining the tenure.
Selecting a Nominee of Choice
A nominee is a person who may receive the policy benefits in case the policyholder loses their life during the policy tenure. The policyholder may nominate one or more nominees. like a family member, friend, etc. Hence, it is one of the essential features of life insurance that needs to be selected carefully.
Maturity Benefits
The maturity benefit is the amount the policyholder draws at the time of policy maturity. This amount may be predetermined at the time of purchasing the life insurance plan (as per the product type) or it may vary based on the investment value (in case of ULIPs). It may consist of any interest or bonus earned on the amount invested.
Claim settlement
The nominee can initiate the claim settlement process to receive the sum assured from the life insurance provider if the policyholder loses their life during the policy term. The claim process of many life insurance companies is quite simple, where the nominee is required to submit all the required documents to receive the benefit under the policy.
Optional Riders
Some life insurance plans come with add-on riders that offer financial benefits over and above those offered under the base plan. These riders can be opted for by paying a nominal additional premium.
Why Having A Life Insurance Policy Is Important?
Once you are aware of the features of life insurance, you may wonder whether you need to buy one.
Read on to understand why life insurance may be important for you:
Financial Security Of Your Loved Ones
Having a life insurance plan may ensure that, in your absence, your family may not have to struggle financially. In case of your sudden demise, your dependents may find themselves in a vulnerable place. During this time, the death benefit that they receive may be crucial in ensuring that they are financially covered.
Protection Against Critical Illnesses
As healthcare costs may continue to skyrocket, it may be important to have a plan in place to cover critical illnesses such as heart attacks and different forms of cancer. These types of illnesses may cause a significant financial burden and may prevent you from earning income while you recover. Life insurance with a critical illness rider may help you during such difficult times.
Achievement of Your Child's Goals
As a parent, you may want to plan ahead for your child’s future needs, including providing for their education, marriage or other goals. In case you lose your life, your life insurance plan may offer a sufficient corpus to take care of your child's needs.
Secure Retirement
Retirement might be a major milestone for some and may require careful financial planning to ensure a comfortable and stress-free life post-retirement. After you stop working, it might be crucial to accumulate enough funds to support your lifestyle. Having a life insurance plan with an investment or saving component may allow you to build a retirement corpus while securing your family.
How to Buy the Right Life Insurance Plan?
Buying life insurance may be an important decision that requires careful consideration. Here is a step-by-step guide that may help you purchase the right life insurance policy:
Assess your Needs
You may start by assessing your financial goals and needs. You may consider factors such as your age, income, liabilities, and dependents. Being aware of your needs may help you determine the life cover you require. You may also use tools like a life insurance calculator to get a better estimate of your coverage needs.
Research Thoroughly
Since there are several types of life insurance plans catering to different needs, you may want to explore different options before narrowing down your choice. You may also consider the premium amount, policy features, riders, and the insurance company’s goodwill before going ahead with the purchase.
Compare Different Plans
After doing your research, you may have shortlisted a few plans that you are considering buying. You can compare different aspects of these plans to find the suitable one for you. You may use online comparison tools or consult an insurance advisor to help you make an informed decision.
Apply for The Policy
Once you've selected a policy, you may fill out the application form and submit the necessary documents, online or offline. The insurance provider will evaluate your application and may ask for additional information, if required.
Make Payment
Choose the suitable premium payment term and frequency. The premium amount may be paid annually, semi-annually, or monthly, depending on the policy type and the insurer.
Choosing a life insurance that suits your financial goals and requirements then may be important. Ensure that you read the fine print and are aware of the terms and conditions of your life insurance plan before going ahead with the purchase.
Frequently Asked Questions about Features of Life Insurance
What are the different types of life insurance?
Term insurance, whole life insurance, money-back insurance, Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP), endowment plan, retirement plan, and child plan are some of the common types of life insurance that most insurance companies may be offering.
Can I buy life insurance riders without buying a base policy?
Riders are the additional benefits that you may be able to add to your base life insurance plan by paying an additional premium. Thus, you may need to purchase a basic life insurance policy in the first place to add a rider. After you purchase a life insurance policy, you may get to choose the riders you wish to purchase. They may be considered add-on benefits to your base plan.
Will I get a return when my life insurance policy matures?
The returns you get if you survive a life insurance policy's tenure may be considered maturity benefits. The feature of maturity benefit may depend on the type of policy you have purchased. For example, a term insurance plan may not have maturity benefits (except term insurance with return of premium), while ULIP may have them.
How long does my life insurance plan last?
Ideally, your life insurance plan lasts for the tenure you select while buying the plan. The policy will stay in force, as long as you pay all due premiums in time.
How many riders can I add to my life insurance policy?
You may add one or all the riders from those offered by your life insurance provider. However, note that for each rider that you add to your plan, you may have to pay an additional premium for it.
References:
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/life/features-of-life-insurance.html
https://www.forbes.com/advisor/in/life-insurance/why-is-life-insurance-important/

The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. Features of Life Insurance
A life insurance plan is a product you may consider when you plan your finances. A life insurance policy may help you in ensuring that your family has the financial support to rely on in case you lose your life. Before buying any financial product, it is essential to note the features it has. Read further to know the common features of life insurance. Important Features of Life Insurance
You may find the characteristics of life insurance plans very simple to understand. Here are its prominent features:
● Issued in the name of the policyholder A policyholder may be considered the entity that purchases a life insurance plan. A policyholder may be an individual, a family, or even a business, depending on the type of policy purchased. ● Premium Payment Options One of the main components of life insurance plan is the premium payable to the insurer. You may choose the premium cycle and payment method based on your convenience. You can choose from several payment options to pay premiums to your insurance provider. ● Selecting the Suitable Sum Assured A sum assured can be considered to be the amount the nominee receives in case the life assured loses their life during the policy term. The life insurance company pays the sum assured to the nominee in the event of the passing away of the life assured during the policy tern, as per the terms and conditions of the plan. The sum assured needs to be selected with care, since it is one of the important factors of life insurance. You may need to consider the financial needs of your loved ones and any liabilities that they may have while choosing the amount of sum assured. ● Option of Choosing the Tenure The policy tenure is the duration for which the policy remains active. The policy term may range from a few years to several decades. You may choose your plan’s duration based on your financial goals and family needs. The policy tenure needs to be selected with care since it may be directly linked to your family's financial security. You may consider your remaining employment years, debts or liabilities, if any, the financial status of dependents, etc. while determining the tenure. ● Selecting a Nominee of Choice
A nominee is a person who may receive the policy benefits in case the life assured loses their life during the policy tenure. You may nominate one or more nominees to receive the underlying benefits of the life insurance policy, in the event of your untimely demise within the policy tenure. Hence, it is one of the essential features of life insurance that needs to be selected carefully. ● Maturity Benefits The maturity benefit is the amount the policyholder draws at the time of policy maturity. This amount may be predetermined at the time of purchasing the life insurance plan (as per the product type) or it may vary based on the investment value (in case of ULIPs). Also depending on the type of the policy, it may consist of bonus (if any) declared by the insurer. ● Claim settlement The nominee can initiate the claim settlement process to receive the sum assured from the life insurance provider if the life assured loses their life during the policy term. The claim process requires the nominee to submit all the required documents to receive the benefit under the policy. ● Optional Riders Some life insurance plans come with add-on riders that offer financial benefits over and above those offered under the base plan. These riders can be opted for by paying a nominal additional premium. Why Having a Life Insurance Policy Is Important?
Once you are aware of the features of life insurance, you may wonder whether you need to buy one. Read on to understand why life insurance may be important for you:
● Financial Security of Your Loved Ones Having a life insurance plan may ensure that, in your absence, your family may not have to struggle financially. In case of your sudden demise, your dependents may find themselves in a vulnerable place. During this time, the death benefit that they receive may be crucial in ensuring that they are financially covered. ● Protection Against Critical Illnesses As healthcare costs may continue to skyrocket, it may be important to have a plan in place to cover critical illnesses such as heart attacks, cancer, etc. These types of illnesses may cause a significant financial burden and may prevent you from earning income while you recover. Life insurance with a critical illness rider may help you during such difficult times. ● Achievement of Your Child's Goals
As a parent, you may want to plan ahead for your child’s future needs, including providing for their education, marriage or other goals. In case you lose your life, your life insurance plan may help in offering a sufficient corpus to take care of your child's financial needs.
● Secure Retirement Retirement might be a major milestone for some and may require careful financial planning to ensure a comfortable and stress-free life post-retirement. After you stop working, it might be crucial to accumulate enough funds to support your lifestyle. Having a life insurance plan with an investment or saving component may allow you to build a retirement corpus while securing your family financially. How to Buy the suitable Life Insurance Plan?
Buying life insurance may be an important decision that requires careful consideration. Here is a step- by-step guide that may help you purchase the suitable life insurance policy:
● Assess your Needs You may start by assessing your financial goals and needs. You may consider factors such as your age, income, liabilities, and dependents. Being aware of your needs may help you determine life cover you require. You may also use tools like a life insurance calculator to get a better estimate of your coverage needs. ● Research Thoroughly Since there are several types of life insurance plans catering to different needs, you may want to explore different options before narrowing down your choice. You may also consider the premium amount, policy features, riders, and the insurance company’s goodwill before going ahead with the purchase. ● Compare Different Plans After doing your research, you may have shortlisted a few plans that you are considering buying. You can compare different aspects of these plans to find the suitable one for you. You may use online comparison tools or consult an insurance advisor to help you make an informed decision. ● Apply for The Policy Once you've selected a policy, you may fill out the application form, also known as proposal form and submit the necessary documents, online or offline. Choose the suitable premium payment term and frequency. The insurance provider will evaluate your application and may ask for additional information, if required. ● Make premium Payment The premium amount may be paid annually, bi-annually, quarterly or monthly, depending on the policy type and the insurer.
Choosing a life insurance plan that suits your financial goals and requirements may be important. Ensure that you read the fine print and are aware of the terms and conditions of your life insurance plan before going ahead with the purchase. Frequently Asked Questions about Life Insurance and its features
● What are the different types of life insurance? Term insurance, whole life insurance, money-back insurance, Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP), endowment plan, retirement plan, and child insurance plan are some of the common types of life insurance plans. ● Can I buy life insurance riders without buying a base policy? Riders are the additional benefits that you may be able to add to your base life insurance plan by paying an additional premium. Thus, you may need to purchase a basic life insurance policy in the first place to add a rider. After you purchase a life insurance policy, you may get to choose the riders you wish to purchase. They may be considered add-on benefits to your base plan. ● Will I get a return when my life insurance policy matures? The benefits received on maturity of a life insurance policy if the life assured survives the policy term, are considered as maturity benefits. The feature of maturity benefit may depend on the type of policy you have purchased. For example, a pure term insurance plan may not have maturity benefits (, while ULIP may have them. ● How long does my life insurance plan last? Ideally, your life insurance plan lasts for the tenure you select while buying the plan. The policy will stay in force, as long as you pay all due premiums on time. ● How many riders can I add to my life insurance policy? You may add one or all the riders from those offered by your life insurance provider under the particular product. However, note that for each rider that you add to your plan, you may have to pay an additional premium for it. Generic References:

The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be
construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
